title: Beer-Lambert Law Calibration Curve Builder
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: AP Chemistry
description: Interactive calibration curve builder that plots absorbance vs. concentration data, reports slope and R^2, and solves for an unknown using Beer-Lambert relationships.

learningObjectives: Construct and interpret a calibration curve to determine molar absorptivity.
Use Beer-Lambert Law to estimate an unknown concentration from measured absorbance.
prerequisites: Definition of absorbance, transmittance, and path length
Ability to perform linear regressions or interpret slope

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Interactive simulation with editable calibration data
keyQuestions: How does the slope of the calibration line relate to molar absorptivity?
What does a low R^2 imply about the reliability of an unknown concentration reading?
commonMisconceptions: Beer-Lambert Law remains perfectly linear at very high concentrations.
Changing the heating rate or measurement order affects molar absorptivity.
assessmentOpportunities: Ask students to diagnose whether a provided dataset violates Beer-Lambert assumptions.
Have students report the concentration of an unknown sample alongside the R^2 to justify confidence.
